text.data-narration: Random access protocols, such as ALOHA, CSMA, and CSMA/CD, allow devices to transmit data randomly without any coordination. If two devices transmit data at the same time, a collision occurs, and the data is lost.
Controlled access protocols, such as Token Ring and Token Bus, ensure that only one device can transmit data at a time, preventing collisions. Examples include Token Ring and Token Bus.

MAC protocols perform the following functions: framing, addressing, error detection and correction, and flow control. Framing involves framing data into packets, adding headers and trailers, while addressing uses MAC addresses to identify devices on a network.

CSMA/CD is a random access protocol used in Ethernet networks, while Token Ring is a controlled access protocol used in ring networks. A token is passed from device to device, allowing devices to transmit data in a controlled manner.

g.text: In conclusion, MAC protocols play a crucial role in managing access to a shared communication medium in a network, ensuring reliable data transfer and preventing collisions.
